message:
chore(deps): bump sha2 0.10.9 → 0.11.0 (semver-major for 0.x)

Migrates fp-conformance to sha2 0.11, which is part of the
`digest-0.11` wave. The public type returned by `Sha256::digest` was
renamed from `GenericArray<u8, U32>` to `Array<u8, U32>` (hybrid-array
crate), and the new `Array` type deliberately no longer implements
`std::fmt::LowerHex`. Call sites using `format!("{:x}", digest)` now
fail to compile.

Two call sites in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:
- `hash_bytes`: `format!("{:x}", Sha256::digest(bytes))`
  → `hex_encode(Sha256::digest(bytes).as_slice())`
- `ArtifactId::short_hash`: same pattern truncated to 8 chars
  → `hex_encode(hash.as_slice())[..8].to_owned()`

Reused the existing `hex_encode` helper (lowercase hex per byte), so
the textual output format is bit-identical to `{:x}`. This preserves
fixture / sidecar / packet-id compatibility.

Cargo.lock now hosts sha2 0.10.9 AND 0.11.0 simultaneously (0.10 still
pulled transitively by asupersync); fp-conformance uses 0.11.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` → OK (compile errors fixed)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fp-conformance --lib` → 334 passed,
  18 failed (identical to baseline — pre-existing env-dependent
  packet/CI-gate failures, unchanged by this bump; hash output format
  parity confirmed by test count unchanged).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
